name: How do I know if my flooring quote is missing hidden costs?

mainEntity.name: How can I tell if a flooring estimate includes hidden costs like leveling, removal, or trim work?
mainEntity.text: How can I tell if a flooring estimate includes hidden costs like leveling, removal, or trim work?

acceptedAnswer.text: The easiest way to spot hidden costs is to look for an itemized estimate. A complete flooring quote should list removal of old flooring, disposal, subfloor repair, leveling, underlayment, moisture barrier, trim work, transitions, stairs, appliance moving, and cleanup separately when applicable. If the quote is only one lump sum, important costs may be hidden until the project is underway. Hidden charges often show up when the installer finds uneven subfloors, damaged trim, or unexpected moisture issues after work begins. That is why a pre-install inspection matters. Ask what assumptions were used in the quote, what would trigger a change order, and whether the price includes finishing details like baseboards and caulk. Comparing quotes line by line is more useful than comparing the total number alone. A low bid can become the most expensive option if it excludes key prep work. Clear scope, not just low price, is what protects the homeowner.
acceptedAnswer.description: A good flooring quote should separate material, labor, demolition, leveling, underlayment, trim, transitions, and cleanup. If those items are not itemized, the estimate may leave room for hidden charges or change orders later.
